name: Elliot Lawrence
abstract: Elliot Lawrence Broza (February 14, 1925 – July 2, 2021), known professionally as Elliot Lawrence, was an American jazz pianist and bandleader. Son of the broadcaster Stan Lee Broza, Lawrence led his first dance band at age 20, but he played swing at the time its heyday was coming to a close.

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Burrell
about_quotes.evidence: I got a call from Elliot Lawrence, bandleader, had a big band... So he asked me if I could play in the band... So I took that and it last about a year and half.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Burrell — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: we were in the band... Elliot let us get the guys we wanted... you know when you have a piano playing leader like that, like Elliot
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Woods
about_quotes.evidence: I got very busy in the studios, yeah, because all the arrangers were still in New York: Quincy was still in New York, Billy Byers, Elliot Lawrence, Al Cohn, uhh Manny Albam, Johnny Richards, ah the list is long, you know, the list is long of the writers
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Woods — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Segal
about_quotes.evidence: So I used to hear that... I knew all the guys: Bill Harris, and all that. I knew who they were, I didn't know them personally.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Segal — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: And the band playing opposite us there was Elliot Lawrence... Oh, they had -- Elliot Lawrence had a tremendous band

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Gibbs
about_quotes.evidence: opposite Elliot Lawrence who was with Les Crane — those shows at that time they all had 16 piece bands.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Gibbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 5, 2021 [interview 2 of 2]
